publicProductsPlansDefined: src/plans.js defines PLANS and per-plan priceEnv/salePriceEnv.
src/server.js line 38 defines publicProductPlanIds as 1day, 3days, monthly, lifetime.
src/server.js lines 767-776 serves database store products at /api/store/products.
src/server.js lines 1199-1285 serves public plan checkout at /api/checkout/create-session.
oldProductsStillReferenced: src/plans.js still defines 2weeks as name Fima Macro 15 Days with STRIPE_PRICE_2WEEKS and STRIPE_SALE_PRICE_2WEEKS.
src/plans.js still defines 1month as duration 30 days with STRIPE_PRICE_1MONTH and STRIPE_SALE_PRICE_1MONTH.
src/plans.js still defines 3months as name Fima Macro 3 Months, duration 90 days, STRIPE_PRICE_3MONTHS, and STRIPE_SALE_PRICE_3MONTHS.
src/adminHtml.js still offers admin direct gift/manual/filter options for 2weeks, 1month, and 3months, with labels including 15 Days and 3 Months.
test/license.test.js still asserts 2weeks, 1month, and 3months expiry behavior.

README.md, DEPLOY_RENDER.md, and .env.example still document old STRIPE_PRICE_1DAY, STRIPE_PRICE_2WEEKS, STRIPE_PRICE_1MONTH, STRIPE_PRICE_3MONTHS and sale env variants including STRIPE_SALE_PRICE_3MONTHS.
freeTrialStripeEnvRequirement: The actual monthly trial claim route in src/server.js lines 1051-1142 creates a license directly and does not call Stripe or read any Stripe price env.

However, src/server.js line 38 still includes 1day in publicProductPlanIds, and src/plans.js lines 2-10 gives 1day priceEnv STRIPE_PRICE_1DAY. A checkout request for plan 1day would still require STRIPE_PRICE_1DAY in production.
inlinePriceDataFallback: src/server.js lines 3759-3835 contains createCheckoutSession fallback to inline price_data.
src/server.js lines 3791-3814 block that fallback in production or live Stripe mode for invalid or missing price env.
Therefore production checkout should not use inline_price_data unless NODE_ENV is not production and STRIPE_MODE is not live.
publicRequiredEnvs: src/server.js lines 125-130 requiredRuntimeEnv only lists STRIPE_PRICE_3DAYS, STRIPE_PRICE_MONTHLY, and STRIPE_PRICE_LIFETIME among public price envs.
But src/server.js lines 38-44 derives stripePriceEnvNames from publicProductPlanIds, which still includes 1day, so startup price validation still includes STRIPE_PRICE_1DAY.
Docs/examples still list old price envs and sale envs.

exactProblemsFound: Legacy plans 2weeks, 1month, and 3months remain in src/plans.js even though public checkout allowlist excludes them.
Old product labels 15 Days, 30-day/1 Month, 90-day/3 Months, 2 Weeks, 3 Months remain in admin UI, tests, docs, and env examples.
Sale price env variants remain documented and configured in plan definitions for legacy products, including STRIPE_SALE_PRICE_3MONTHS.
1day is still treated as a public product plan in src/server.js, causing checkout validation and checkout creation to involve STRIPE_PRICE_1DAY even though requiredRuntimeEnv no longer includes it.
Production checkout blocks inline_price_data fallback correctly, but the fallback code still exists for non-production/non-live modes.
Runtime required envs are the intended three price envs, but derived validation and docs are not fully aligned.
nextRecommendedStep: Edit only the payment/product foundation files to remove legacy public checkout products and old env documentation, keep Free Trial off Stripe checkout, and align derived public price validation to STRIPE_PRICE_3DAYS, STRIPE_PRICE_MONTHLY, and STRIPE_PRICE_LIFETIME.
